title: paysonia lyrata
intro: Paysonia lyrata, also known as buck's horn plantain or green-headed coneflower, is a perennial plant species native to the eastern United States. It has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infection. The plant's extracts have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Paysonia lyrata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used to support the immune system, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations.
